utility_evaluation.REASONING: There are 5 items requested in the required format: health_coverage, property_coverage, travel_coverage, additional_services, total_budget. In the final coverage plan, all 5 items appear. Now check if each is fully specified with all required details: The format requires each item to include coverage and costs. The plan provides a description and a cost for each of the five items. Health: includes description for physical and mental health and a monthly cost. Property: includes home/contents with components and a monthly cost. Travel: includes travel insurance details and a monthly cost. Additional services: lists services and states costs are included in monthly premiums. Total budget: provides a consolidated monthly cost. Thus, all 5 items are present and have costs specified.

llm_ratings_evaluation.REASONING: The final coverage plan mentions specific product names that appear in the ground-truth ratings. Map each mentioned item to its exact counterpart and rating: Health uses PrimeCare Premium Plan (primary_health_coverage.physical_health -> rating 6, and mental_health PrimeCare Premium Plan exists with rating 4, but the plan bundles 'Physical & Mental Health' under PrimeCare Premium; the closest explicit match in ratings is PrimeCare Premium Plan under both categories. Since the plan lists one combined PrimeCare Premium Plan, we map to the PrimeCare Premium Plan rating from primary health physical, the clearest reference). Property lists EliteHome Premium Coverage (rating 6) along with additional bundled features; only EliteHome is in the ratings. Travel lists PrimeTravel Premium Plan (rating 6) and an Adventure Travel add-on (Adventure Travel Coverage, rating 10). Additional services are generic descriptions overlapping multiple services, but not exact named items in ground-truth beyond those already mapped.
